prompt: Running 'java Greet' with no arguments throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException. What is the bug?
choices: args is null when no arguments are passed
args[0] is accessed without checking args.length — if no arguments are given the array is empty and index 0 is out of bounds
String[] args must be initialized before use in main()
Command-line arguments must be integers; Strings require explicit parsing
explanation: When no arguments are passed, args is a non-null but empty array (length 0). Accessing args[0] on an empty array throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException. Fix: check args.length before accessing — if (args.length < 1) { System.out.println("usage: Greet <name>"); return; }
